title: Level 216
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Almendra'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Almendra'}} {{Background|file=IlluminatedTunnel.jpg}}
{{Headerbox|file=IlluminatedTunnel.jpg|header=The ceiling drips...}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 216 : "Mystical Castle"}} {{Alt Class|ent=2|no=216|ext=3|env=3}} '''Level 216''' is the 217th level of '''The Backrooms'''.
General Description
'''Level 216''', often referred to as "Mystical Castle," possesses an architectural style reminiscent of a grand castle renovated into a structure serving a different purpose. This level is confined in size, measuring approximately 600 meters in width, 400 meters in length, and 200 meters in height. Despite its dimensions, the design of this castle is unique for each visitor; it undergoes alterations with every new arrival; therefore, it is impossible to map out this level or form bases on this level. Unlike traditional castles, there is no obvious entrance to be found. The temperature of the level stays at a constant low temperature. If not equipped with many clothes, wanderers may experience an unpleasant cold chill and significantly slower movement due to the freezing sensation. These temperatures are almost always constant, at 6 °C, or 42.8 °F. {{Blurimage|file=IlluminatedTunnel.jpg|caption=An image of a hallway in this level.}} The castle's layout is a labyrinth of serpentine hallways, occasionally branching into chambers or staircases. The majority of these corridors boast an expansive ceiling that opens up to the visible sky. Constructed primarily from weathered stones, these hallways have been coated with a shade of pale-yellow paint. Throughout the structure, numerous arches and support beams are prominently displayed, bearing the weight of the imposing ceiling. This ceiling poses an environmental hazard to the wanderer; there is a slim possibility of it collapsing if it lacks sufficient support, potentially occurring while the wanderer is below. The walls are lined with small, dim lights and metallic pipes; these pipes are strictly advised not to tinker with as they are thought to contain an extremely toxic gas within them. If one happens to damage these pipes and cause a hole, then the gas will instantly leak out at a constant and fast pace. The staircases, occasionally branching from the castle's main corridors, are typically towering structures that span from the depths of the castle to the very top of the castle. Oddly enough, when the wanderer gazes in a direction contrary to their intended path—be it downward or upward—their vision becomes obscured and dimmed. This phenomenon is harmless and does not occur as long as one avoids looking away from their desired direction of travel. Similarly, cameras experience this peculiar effect when attempting to capture images or footage; however, this only occurs when the camera is pointed downward. This may cause disorientation for any wanderers who attempt to film the level due to this strange effect.{{Blurimage|file=BlurredStaircase.png|caption=An image with the camera receiving the effect.|align=left}} Several of the staircases descend into the castle's basement, an area where the walls are visibly ravaged, composed of brittle stones, and shrouded in verdant moss. Venturing into the basement is strongly discouraged due to its precarious stability, posing a greater risk of collapse compared to the upper levels. Moreover, the air quality is notably poor; reports indicate that individuals who remain in the basement for approximately 10 minutes experience respiratory difficulties and soon thereafter suffer from severe illness. However, a benefit of the basement is that you have a guaranteed chance of finding vital resources for survival. Excluding the basement, the staircases within the castle provide access to various floors and occasionally to medium-sized chambers. These chambers mirror the hallways in design, featuring consistent walls with the same pale-yellow painted cobblestone walls and lined lighting, and are occasionally furnished with wooden objects such as chairs, cabinets, and tables. However, they are prone to water leaks of an undetermined origin. As a result, these rooms may become excessively moist over time, eventually emitting a disagreeable scent and potentially disorienting wanderers. It is advisable to leave this level promptly to avoid such discomfort.
''A mysterious note found by an unknown wanderer.''
''"So, I have recently attempted to make my way outside the castle. I have made many attempts, and only a few were just a bit close. But on one of my close attempts, I was able to have a short glimpse at what exists outside of the castle, and it was something else. What I saw was rather indescribable; I don't even remember it that way anymore, but it was quite magical.''
Anomalies
After staying at this level for a prolonged period, many changes can occur to the level. Over time, more effects will occur, and they will greatly worsen over time. However, these effects do not happen dangerously fast, and they seem to take a long enough time for the wanderer to safely leave the level. ===''-After 20 hours.-''=== After 20 hours, slight effects might be noticeable. These effects are listed below. *Staircases may randomly stop existing or shift to a different location. *The walls, floors, and ceilings, as well as the furniture at this level, appear to mysteriously be significantly more stained with a dirt-like substance. ===''-After 45 hours.-''=== After 45 hours, the effects seem to worsen, and a few others are introduced. *Staircases now only stop existing and do not change location, reducing the total number of staircases. *The level begins to darken slightly. The sunlight becomes weaker, and so do the lights placed around the level. *The smell of cut grass is more noticeable around the level. ===''-After 70 hours.-''=== After 70 hours, the effects start to become significantly more dangerous. *The level begins to darken even further, and the lights placed around the level begin to vanish. *The smell of cut grass transforms into an unpleasant smell, this is linked to the chambers which over time get more moist and begin to release an unpleasant and disorientating odor. *The chances of the ceiling collapsing greatly increases, aswell as the chance that it will happen to collapse while the wanderer is directly below. *The amount of entities in the level increases, from what would be a 2/5 in classification, to a 4/5 in classification. ===''-After 90 hours.-''=== After 90 hours, the effects are extremely dangerous at this point, and are also at the point of where they stop worsening. *Almost no sunlight is coming from openings in the ceiling, as if it were to be night time. The lights around the level also are mostly taken out, and can rarely be found turned on. *The odor will worsen to a point where the wanderer might vomit, aswell as might develop a sickness. *The entities present on the level will become more aggressive than they should be, and will relentlessly attempt anything in order to kill you.
Entities
Entities are most commonly found within rooms of this level, and are not usually found roaming in the hallways. They will become more aggressive over time and increase in quantity as mentioned above, but they will begin as being slightly less hostile than they would usually be. The entities in this level also have slightly altered behaviors from their usual behavior. Certain areas can have certain entities, which will be listed below. *[[Entity 6|Dullers]] are the most common entities found in this level. They mostly roam around the medium-sized chambers that are found to sometimes be connected to the main hallways of this level. They usually prefer to hide if they notice a wanderer, and attack unexpectedly. *[[Entity 3|Smilers]] are less common to find, but are still found in quite a few places within '''Level 216'''. They are mostly found in groups of 3 or more, and infact are less hostile the larger the pack of '''Smilers''' is. There is no specific area in which '''Smilers''' can be found, meaning that they are practically evenly spread out through the level. *[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]] are the most uncommon entities to encounter within this level. They usually can be found roaming around the lower floors of this level, aswell as the basement of this level. Their behavior in this level does not differ much from its usual behavior unlike any other entities found in this level.
Entrances and Exits
===Entrances=== *Entering any castle-like structure on [[Level 10]] or any of its sub-levels will lead one to here. *If for any reason, the ceiling of [[Level 2]] starts to leak and not stop, you can attempt to no-clip through the ceiling to get here. *Following a moving container containing salts in [[Level 620]] may take one here. ===Exits=== *If a wall is seen with thick roots growing out of them, no-clipping through them will take you to [[Level 45]]. *Hallways that begin to take the appearance of a cave will lead to [[Level 8]]. *The staircases of this level may have doors found within them, these are usually locked but if not then it will lead to either [[Level 5]] or [[Level 21]]. {{Redirect|Prev=215|Next=217}} {{Collapse|num=216|initial=Open Credits|final=Close Credits|body= Author: [[User:Skepnik|Skepnik]] Photographer: [[User:Taverity|Taverity]] }} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Environmental Hazards]] {{CSS|content= :root { --theme-page-background-color--secondary:rgba(0,0,0,0.8); --theme-page-background-color:rgba(0,0,0,0.3); }}
